title: draba subumbellata
intro: Draba subumbellata, also known as Drummond's rock cress, is a perennial plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Draba subumbellata include its potential to treat various conditions such as bronchitis, fever, and skin infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Draba subumbellata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
